title: Kentucky man arrested in Mass. gun store robbery
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Kentucky man Aaron Woodrum, 33, arrested for breaking into Norwood's Liberty Ordnance Supply, stealing firearms.
Police tracked Woodrum using a K-9 unit to a home where stolen goods and suspect's clothing were found.
Facing multiple charges, Woodrum is held on $100,000 bail with a probable cause hearing set for June 17.

title: Canton vocational school teacher on leave following ‘reported allegations’
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Blue Hills Regional Technical School in Canton has placed a vocational teacher on leave amid 'reported allegations.'
Superintendent Jill M. Rossetti said the teacher was immediately placed on administrative leave after concerns were reported.
Canton police confirmed their investigation into the matter but have not provided further details about the allegations.

title: More than 10,000 votes cast: This is the best beach town in New England
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Boston.com readers voted Ogunquit, Maine as New England's best beach town in a contest with over 10,000 votes.
Ogunquit beat Bar Harbor in the final round by 468 votes, following a bracket that started with 32 towns.
Ogunquit is noted for its picturesque scenery, friendly vibe, and top travel rankings, according to town manager Matthew Buttrick.

title: Police looking for suspect after man struck with beer bottle near Southie bar
author: John Waller
bullets: Boston police seek help identifying a suspect who struck a man with a beer bottle near Lincoln Tavern.
Incident occurred around 12:30 a.m. on May 9 after suspect made lewd gestures at victim's girlfriend.
Suspect is described as a white male, 26-30 years old, 5'9"-5'10", with dark hair and a beard.

title: Tedy Bruschi made a cameo appearance in a Rhode Island local news segment
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Tedy Bruschi appeared in a local NBC 10 news segment while preparing to fish in North Kingstown, Rhode Island.
NBC 10 meteorologist A.J. Mastrangelo spotted Bruschi, noting his outfit was suited for water temperatures on a hot day.
Bruschi joked about not revealing his fishing spots while mentioning he planned to fish for tautog.

title: Driver dead after truck crashes into South Shore home
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A 58-year-old man died after his pickup truck crashed into a Hanson home on Monday evening.
The crash occurred at 481 High St. around 6:20 p.m., with the truck rolling onto its side.
Emergency personnel extricated the driver using hydraulic tools before he was pronounced dead at Brockton Hospital.

title: Local obituary: Elizabeth ‘Lizzie’ Dankert, 24, Central Catholic and Union College alum
author: Boston.com Staff
bullets: Elizabeth 'Lizzie' Dankert, 24, from Boston, passed away on May 14, 2026, leaving a legacy of love and joy.
Lizzie, a Central Catholic and Union College alumna, excelled in soccer and worked in sales at PTC.
A Funeral Mass is on May 21 at St. Augustine’s Church, Andover, with visitation on May 20 at Conte Funeral Home.

title: Don’t mess with the nest: Goose expert responds to viral Boston goose attack video
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A viral TikTok video showing a goose attacking a dog in Boston has over 6.7 million views.
Researcher Mikaela Higgs explains the goose was protecting its young during the spring nesting season.
Higgs advises keeping a safe distance from geese, as they can be defensive near their goslings.

title: Jackson Mann site could include housing, study finds ahead of community meeting
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: A city-commissioned study supports including housing in the Jackson Mann Community Center redevelopment in Allston.
Mayor Wu allocated $10 million for the design phase of Jackson Mann's redevelopment in her five-year capital budget.
A community meeting to discuss Jackson Mann's future will be held at the center on May 28 at 6 p.m.

title: Here’s how hot meteorologists say it could get in Boston this week
author: Carson Lyle and Heather Alterisio
bullets: Boston is under a heat advisory from 11 a.m. Tuesday to 8 p.m. Wednesday due to record-breaking temperatures.
Temperatures are expected to reach the mid-90s, challenging record highs for mid-May in the Boston area.
Meteorologists forecast the weather to remain mostly sunny with possible afternoon thunderstorms through Wednesday.
